english: Glr. — blo °krid-pa (blo 'khrid pa) perh. a mistake for brid-pa (brid pa).

1. to wind or coil round (of serpents), to embrace closely, to clasp round, e.g. in the act of coition; ma byams bú-la °kril (ma byams bu la 'khril) a loving mother clasping her child Pth.; °kril-mkan ('khril mkhan) a plant furnished with tendrils or claspers W.; °kril-śiṅ ('khril shing) Wdn. a climbing plant, creeper. — 2. to glide, slip into, of the soul when entering another body, = °kyúd-pa ('khyud pa). — 3. ka °kril-ba (kha 'khril ba) W. to speak imperfectly (like children), to stammer, — 4. to heap up, = °dril-ba ('dril ba), sgril-ba (sgril ba).

1. syn. with gram (gram), bank, shore, coast, rmá-čui °kris-na yód-pai mkár (rma chu'i 'khris na yod pa'i mkhar), a castle on the banks of the Hoangho Glr.; *kyo'-ráṅ-gi ṭi-na yo* (khyo' rang gi ti na yo) C. it lies just before you, under your nose; blá-mai sku-kris-su (bla ma'i sku khris su) = blá-mai pyógs-la (bla ma'i phyogs la) Mil.nt. — 2. v. °kri-ba ('khri ba).
-1. Cs. to wash, to bathe, = °krud-pa ('khrud pa), cf. krus (khrus). — 2. diarrhoea, looseness; dysentery (?); °kru-nád ('khru nad), °kru-sbyóṅs ('khru sbyongs) (अतिसार) id.

1. vb., pf. °krugs ('khrugs), cf. dkrug-pa (dkrug pa), bkrug-pa (bkrug pa), to be in disorder, agitation, commotion, to be disturbed; °krúg-par gyúr-ba ('khrug par gyur ba) to get disordered; of the blood: rtsa tams-cád °krúg-tu bcug (rtsa thams cad 'khrug tu bcug), it made all his blood boil Glr.; of the sea trq.; esp. of the mind, disturbed by wrath, fear, anxiety, or some other passion, cf. kog-°krúgs (khog 'khrugs); to quarrel, fight, contend, de gnyis °krúgs-nas (de gnyis 'khrugs nas), the two quarrelling; bod ce naṅ °krúg-go (bod ce nang 'khrug go), the nobles of Tibet are contending among one another, have internal feuds; mčí-ma °krúg-pa (mchi ma 'khrug pa) tears appearing, coming forth, (lit. tears being stirred up, excited Thgy., Mil., Tar. — 2. sbst. disorder, tumult, war, also single combat, duel, °krúg-pa śor ('khrug pa shor) disorder arises; °krug-dús byas ('khrug dus byas) he appointed the time of the duel Glr.; °krúg-dpon ('khrug dpon) = dmág-dpon (dmag dpon); °krúg-pa byed-pa ('khrug pa byed pa) to take up arms, to begin war; respecting subjects: to rebel; °krúg-pa byéd-pai dús-su ('khrug pa byed pa'i dus su) in times of war Glr.; dmag-°krúg (dmag 'khrug), tab-°krúg (thab 'khrug) war. — mi-°krúgs-pa (mi 'khrugs pa) n. of a Buddha (not = mi-skyód-pa (mi bskyod pa)). — °krug-lóṅ ('khrug long) is the explanation given by Lexx. for skyo-ṅógs (skyo ngogs), hence prob.: contest, strife. — *túgs-mkan* (thugs mkhan) W. having small cracks, flaws, of potter's ware.

1. resp. for skyé-ba (skye ba) to be born, bcom-ldan-dás °kruṅs-pa daṅ dus-mnyám-du (bcom ldan 'das 'khrungs pa dang dus mnyam du) at the same time when Buddha was born Glr.; gnyis-la sras ma °kruṅs-par (gnyis la sras ma 'khrungs par) as by neither of the two (queens) a son was born Glr.; °kruṅs-dkái skyés-bu ('khrungs dka'i skyes bu) (holy) men, such as are but rarely born (lit. with difficulty) Mil.; to arise, to originate, °kruṅs-rábs ('khrungs rabs) legend of the origin...; kyed-ráṅ-gi túgs-la °kruṅs-pai tsig (khyed rang gi thugs la 'khrungs pa'i tshig) words as they may just arise in your honour's mind Mil.; snyin-rǰe túgs-la °kruṅs-pas (snying rje thugs la 'khrungs pas) compassion arose in the soul of his reverence Mil.; tiṅ-ṅe-dzin °kruṅs-pas (ting nge 'dzin 'khrungs pas) meditation arising. — 2. to come up, shoot, sprout, grow, of seeds and plants frq.

pf. bkrus (bkrus), fut. bkru (bkru) to wash, to bathe, gos (gos) clothes, ka-lág (kha lag) face and hands Dzl.; to wash off, dri-ma (dri ma) dirt; fig. sa náṅ-gyis °krud (sa nang gyis 'khrud) Ma. is stated to mean: the country is fleeced, thoroughly drained of its resources.
carcass, carrion, game torn by beasts of prey, Sch., (the word seems to be very little known).

(Lex.: Ssk. भ्रम (bhrama) to turn out of the way, to wander, to stray, hence perh. originally:) 1. to be dislocated, sprained, distorted, *tsig ṭul* (tshig 'khrul) W. the limb is dislocated; usually: 2. to be out, to be mistaken, almost always used in the pf tense, °krúl-pa ('khrul pa) mistaken, deceived, ṅa mig °krúl-pa yin-nam (nga mig 'khrul pa yin nam) does my eye deceive me? Mil.; rná-ba °krul (rna ba 'khrul) dogs túr-re gyis (dogs thur re gyis) take care not to hear wrong Mil.; gnyis gcig-tu °krúl-bar byéd-pa (gnyis gcig tu 'khrul bar byed pa) to make by mistake two to be one, to confound one thing with another Tar.; sti dge-slóṅ-mar dód-pa °krúl-pa yín-la (sti dge slong mar 'dod pa 'khrul pa yin la) she being frustrated in her wish to become a nun Tar. 85, 1; gró-ba °krúl-pa ('gro ba 'khrul pa) the deceived creature Glr.;
